model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 training steps (well under one epoch), increasing Adam's learning rate is the highest-leverage low-complexity change to improve early optimization speed and reduce validation loss within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current configuration appears too aggressive early; reducing LR back to 5e-4 while keeping the fixed-step cosine schedule should improve stability and validation loss within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Under a very short 256-step budget, regularization can overconstrain learning. Removing weight decay while reverting the scheduler to the previously stronger unscheduled regime should improve early fit and potentially reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Now that weight decay is removed, a modest LR increase may improve progress within 256 steps without the instability seen at 1e-3.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The slight degradation at 6e-4 suggests the optimum is lower. Trying 4e-4 is a focused local sweep around the current best regime (no weight decay, no schedule) to improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Recent results indicate the best region shifted downward; testing 3e-4 extends the successful LR sweep to find a better point under the same short-step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed training steps, increasing batch size exposes the model to more unique examples per step and can improve validation loss; restoring 4e-4 keeps LR in the best-performing local range.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given the strong gain from increasing batch size under fixed-step training, testing 256 is a direct continuation that may further improve validation loss by covering more data per step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The trend has been strongly positive with larger batches in fixed-step mode; pushing to 512 is the highest-leverage next micro tweak to increase data coverage per update.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling continues to deliver large gains in this fixed-step setting; testing 1024 is the next direct extension to maximize sample throughput per optimizer update.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After large gains from increased effective data throughput, the next likely bottleneck is model capacity; a moderate width increase should improve representational power without changing training logic.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing capacity, a slightly higher learning rate can improve progress over the same fixed number of updates; this is a minimal local retune around the current strong configuration.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Larger batches have been the strongest lever throughout this run under fixed-step evaluation; scaling from 1024 to 2048 is the most promising next move.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size now very large (2048), increasing learning rate is the most direct next step to match the larger-batch optimization regime and potentially improve loss within the same fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance is still improving as LR increases in the large-batch regime; 1e-3 is the next reasonable step to test for additional fixed-step optimization gains.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss kept improving as LR increased at batch size 2048, so a modest further increase to 1.2e-3 is a targeted continuation of the same successful trend.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Learning-rate gains are tapering, so increasing hidden-layer expansion is a targeted capacity upgrade that can improve fit while keeping the training loop unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Recent changes continue to improve loss incrementally; a final local LR increase is the most efficient remaining sweep in this large-batch, higher-capacity regime.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last increase overshot; stepping LR down to 1.1e-3 keeps the strong large-batch/high-capacity regime while targeting a better stability-performance tradeoff for the final trial.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}
